one_time_description: Contribution to Saulteaux First Nation - Sewage Pumping Station located on Saulteaux First Nation land, transferred as a non-recurring capital contribution
economic_notes: First Nation has an investment in Moosomin First Nation Economic Development Company Limited Partnership (GBE), which holds 50% interest in Western Alliance Tubulars Inc. (oil and gas products supplier/manufacturer). Significant advances of $3,446,967 outstanding to Western Alliance Tubulars Inc. with measurement uncertainty. COVID-19 noted as significant event with potential impact on casino revenues (BATC/SIGA), trust revenues, and Western Alliance Tubulars Inc. collections.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (March 31, 2020). Nation is located in Saskatchewan (Treaty 6 Territory), auditor signed in Saskatoon SK. Annual surplus is a deficit of $34,126 due to $1,957,031 one-time contribution of Sewage Pumping Station to Saulteaux First Nation. GBE (Moosomin First Nation Economic Development Company Limited Partnership) accounted for by modified equity method with negative equity of $196,752. Significant measurement uncertainty on $3,446,967 advances to Western Alliance Tubulars Inc. highlighted in Emphasis of Matter paragraph. COVID-19 noted as significant subsequent event. Consolidated entities include Moosomin First Nation Education Council Inc. and Moosomin First Nation C.M.H.C. Housing Projects. Moosomin 1909 Surrender Trust excluded as not controlled. No remuneration schedule included in provided text. ISC total government transfers calculated as ISC $10,226,934 + CMHC $641,149 + BATC $344,663 + SITAG $179,464 + Battle River Treaty 6 Health Centre $160,406 + First Nations Trust $591,361 = $12,143,977; however First Nations Trust may be non-government; conservative estimate used. osr calculated as total revenue minus ISC funding minus CMHC minus other identifiable government transfers.
